title: PIVOT: Plan-Inspect-eVOlve Trajectory Optimization for Self-Correcting Agents
description: PIVOT treats agent trajectories as optimizable objects, using natural-language feedback as surrogate textual gradients. It identifies the earliest divergence between planned and executed trajectories, repairs only the unsupported suffix (local repair, not full regeneration), and applies a monotonic acceptance rule so solution quality never degrades across iterations.
workflow: PLAN: Generate one or more candidate action trajectories for the task
INSPECT: Execute each trajectory in a sandbox and compute structured textual gradients encoding plan-vs-execution discrepancies
EVOLVE: Using the discrepancy signals, repair only the unsupported suffix after the earliest divergence point — never regenerate the full plan
VERIFY: Run a final global constraint check on the evolved trajectory; accept only if it improves over the previous best (monotonic acceptance)
RETRY: If verification fails or constraints are unsatisfied, loop back to EVOLVE with the updated gradient signals; exit after max iterations or acceptance

title: ReAct Agent Loop: Reason-Act-Observe for Autonomous Tool-Using Agents
description: The ReAct pattern interleaves reasoning (thought) and acting (tool calls) in an observe-think-act loop. The agent generates a thought tracing its reasoning, selects and invokes a tool, observes the result, then decides whether to continue or return a final answer. This is the foundational pattern used by LangGraph, OpenHands, and most modern agent frameworks.
workflow: RECEIVE: Parse the user query into the agent's initial state
THINK: The LLM generates a reasoning trace (thought) about what to do next
ACT: If the thought indicates a tool call, invoke the specified tool with the given arguments and capture the observation
OBSERVE: Append the tool result (or direct LLM response) to the message history
DECIDE: If the last message contains tool calls, route back to ACT; if it contains a final answer, route to END; enforce a recursion_limit to prevent infinite loops

title: Agentic RAG with Evidence Sufficiency Gating
description: Modern agentic RAG systems dynamically decide when to retrieve, how much to retrieve, and when to stop retrieving by evaluating evidence sufficiency. Instead of single-shot retrieval, the agent iteratively gathers evidence, assesses whether it has enough to answer confidently, and only commits to an answer when the evidence passes a quality threshold.
workflow: RECEIVE: Parse the user query and identify sub-questions that require external evidence
RETRIEVE: For each sub-question, generate targeted search queries and retrieve documents from the knowledge base
ASSESS: Evaluate retrieved evidence for relevance, grounding, and adequacy — use an Evidence Sufficiency Controller or Triple-Check verification
COMMIT or RETRY: If evidence is sufficient, synthesize an answer; if gaps remain, generate new retrieval queries targeting the gaps and loop back
SYNTHESIZE: Merge all sub-answers into a coherent response with citations; flag any remaining uncertainty

title: Claude Code Parallel Worktree Agent Orchestration
description: Claude Code supports five levels of parallelism: subagents (delegated workers inside one session), agent view (background session dispatch), agent teams (coordinated sessions with shared task lists), worktrees (isolated git checkouts for conflict-free parallel work), and /batch (automated PR generation across worktrees). Worktrees are the key primitive that makes multi-agent file editing safe.
workflow: PLAN: Break the task into independent subtasks that can run in parallel without file-level conflicts
DISPATCH: Launch each subtask as a separate Claude Code session with `claude --worktree <name>`, creating isolated git checkouts under `.claude/worktrees/`
EXECUTE: Each worktree session independently reads, edits, tests, and commits changes on its own branch — no merge conflicts during execution
REVIEW: Use `claude agents` to monitor all background sessions; review each worktree's changes before merging
MERGE: Merge completed worktree branches back into the main branch; auto-cleanup removes empty worktrees, dirty ones prompt keep-or-discard

title: Production Agent Monitoring and Graceful Degradation Pipeline
description: A production-ready pipeline for autonomous agents that monitors decision quality (not just uptime), enforces per-agent token budgets, implements model-fallback chains, and provides kill switches for runaway agents. Based on SRE practices adapted for AI agent systems, including judgment SLOs and behavioral chaos testing.
workflow: INSTRUMENT: Emit OpenTelemetry spans for every agent decision, tool call, and token spend; define judgment SLOs (decision quality, not just latency/uptime)
BUDGET: Set per-agent and per-operation-class token budgets; track token_cost_per_task as a leading indicator — a 40% spike precedes visible quality degradation by 24-48 hours

DEGRADE: Implement a 4-level fallback chain: (1) retry with exponential backoff, (2) model fallback (Opus -> Sonnet -> Haiku), (3) queue and defer non-urgent tasks, (4) graceful decline with user notification
RESPOND: When an alert fires (cost spike, quality drop, loop detection), execute the runbook: kill the runaway agent, fall back to cheaper model, notify the oncall, and preserve forensic traces
LEARN: Run weekly behavioral chaos tests (inject malformed tool responses, contradictory instructions) to surface failure modes before they hit production

title: HECG Hierarchical Error-Correcting Agent Loop for Embodied Tasks
description: HECG reformulates plan execution as a graph-based, feedback-driven process with three hierarchical correction levels: L1 (local parameter adjustment), L2 (option switching to an alternative action), and L3 (full task re-planning preserving failure history). A fourth level escalates to human-in-the-loop for safety-critical situations.
workflow: PLAN: Generate an action sequence using an LLM planner with error history context
EXECUTE: Execute the next action in the sequence within a simulated or physical environment
L1-CORRECT: If a small continuous error is detected (e.g., misaligned gripper), adjust parameters and retry the same action
L2-SWITCH: If L1 corrections fail repeatedly, select an alternative action from the candidate set (e.g., push instead of grasp)
L3-REPLAN: If L2 also fails or a cascading error occurs, regenerate the entire remaining action sequence, preserving failure history for context
L4-ESCALATE: If the task is safety-critical or all correction levels fail, pause execution and escalate to a human operator

title: LangGraph ReAct Agent with Tool Calling (Python)
description: A minimal but complete ReAct agent built with LangGraph v1.0 that uses the StateGraph API, ToolNode for parallel tool execution, and conditional routing between the LLM and tool nodes. Demonstrates the core observe-think-act loop with proper edge routing and recursion limits.
code:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from langgraph.graph.message import MessagesState
from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.tools import tool

@tool
def multiply(a: float, b: float) -> float:
    """Multiply two numbers together."""
    return a * b

@tool
def add(a: float, b: float) -> float:
    """Add two numbers together."""
    return a + b

tools = [multiply, add]
model_with_tools = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini").bind_tools(tools)
tool_node = ToolNode(tools)

def call_model(state: MessagesState) -> dict:
    response = model_with_tools.invoke(state["messages"])
    return {"messages": [response]}

def should_continue(state: MessagesState) -> str:
    last_msg = state["messages"][-1]
    if hasattr(last_msg, "tool_calls") and last_msg.tool_calls:
        return "tools"
    return "__end__"

builder = StateGraph(MessagesState)
builder.add_node("agent", call_model)
builder.add_node("tools", tool_node)
builder.add_edge(START, "agent")
builder.add_conditional_edges("agent", should_continue, {
    "tools": "tools",
    "__end__": END
})
builder.add_edge("tools", "agent")
graph = builder.compile()

result = graph.invoke({"messages": [("user", "What is 47 * 83?")]})
print(result["messages"][-1].content)

title: CrewAI Autonomous Research Crew (Python)
description: A CrewAI example defining a multi-agent crew with role-based agents, sequential task delegation, and tool integration. Each agent has a specialized role, goal, and backstory, and tasks are executed in order with context passing between them.
code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool

search_tool = SerperDevTool()

researcher = Agent(
    role="Senior Research Analyst",
    goal="Discover and analyze cutting-edge AI research",
    backstory="You are an experienced researcher who finds the most relevant and recent information.",
    tools=[search_tool],
    verbose=True,
    max_iter=5,
    respect_context_window=True,
)

writer = Agent(
    role="Tech Content Writer",
    goal="Transform research into clear, engaging articles",
    backstory="You are a skilled writer who makes complex topics accessible.",
    verbose=True,
    max_iter=3,
)

research_task = Task(
    description="Research the latest developments in autonomous AI agents.",
    expected_output="A structured summary with 5 key findings and sources.",
    agent=researcher,
)

write_task = Task(
    description="Write a compelling article based on the research findings.",
    expected_output="A 500-word article with introduction, key points, and conclusion.",
    agent=writer,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, writer],
    tasks=[research_task, write_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
    verbose=True,
)

result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

title: Self-Debugging Agent with OpenHands SDK (Python)
description: Using the OpenHands SDK V1 to create a self-debugging code agent that writes code, runs tests, observes failures, and iteratively corrects its own output. Demonstrates the action-execution-observation loop with event-sourced state management.
code:
from openhands.sdk import Agent, ConversationState, LLM, Tool
from openhands.sdk.tools import BashTool, FileEditTool

llm = LLM(model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514")
tools = [BashTool(), FileEditTool()]

agent = Agent(
    name="self_debugging_coder",
    llm=llm,
    tools=tools,
    instructions="""You are a coding agent. Write code, run tests, and fix failures iteratively.
    1. Read the problem description.
    2. Write initial implementation.
    3. Run the provided tests.
    4. If tests fail, read the error output and fix the code.
    5. Repeat until all tests pass or max 3 iterations.""",
)

state = ConversationState()
state.add_user_message("Write a Python function that finds the longest palindromic substring. Include tests.")

for i in range(5):  # max iterations
    action = agent.get_next_action(state)
    if action is None:
        break
    observation = action.execute(state)
    state.add_observation(observation)
    if state.has_final_answer():
        break

print(state.final_answer)

title: MCP Tool Definition for Autonomous Agents (TypeScript)
description: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes tools for autonomous agents to discover and use at runtime. Demonstrates the tool definition pattern that enables dynamic tool discovery, progressive disclosure, and code-mode execution — reducing token consumption by up to 98.7% compared to loading all tool definitions upfront.

title: Browser Automation Agent with Playwright MCP (TypeScript)
description: An autonomous browser agent that uses Playwright MCP to navigate web pages, fill forms, extract data, and take actions through structured accessibility snapshots rather than screenshots. Demonstrates the observe-think-act loop for web tasks with token-efficient accessibility tree parsing.

title: PreFlect: From Retrospective to Prospective Reflection in LLM Agents
description: PreFlect shifts agent reflection from post-hoc correction to pre-execution foresight. By distilling recurring failure patterns from historical trajectories and critiquing plans before execution, it achieves 59.39% on GAIA with Gemini-2.5-pro, outperforming more complex multi-agent architectures by 3%+.

title: SPIRAL: Symbolic LLM Planning via Grounded and Reflective Search
description: SPIRAL embeds three specialized LLM agents (Planner, Simulator, Critic) into a Monte Carlo Tree Search loop. The Planner proposes creative next steps, the Simulator predicts realistic outcomes, and the Critic provides dense reward signals through reflection. Achieves 83.6% on DailyLifeAPIs, 16+ points over the next-best search framework.

title: Evaluator-Optimizer Pattern: Generate-Evaluate-Loop for Autonomous Agents
description: The evaluator-optimizer pattern uses one LLM to generate content and another to evaluate it against quality criteria. The loop continues until the evaluator certifies the output meets the threshold. This is the canonical pattern for self-correcting agents in LangGraph, where a structured output validator serves as the evaluator and the generator is the optimizer.

title: LangChain Deep Agents from Scratch Course
description: Official LangChain Academy course teaching three deep agent patterns: task planning with TODO lists and status tracking, context offloading to virtual file systems to manage token budgets, and context isolation via sub-agent delegation. Includes 5 progressive notebooks culminating in a full research agent with web search and LangGraph Studio integration.

title: LangGraph v1.0: StateGraph API for Agent Orchestration
description: LangGraph v1.0 provides the StateGraph API for building cyclic agent graphs with persistence, streaming, debugging, and deployment. It supports conditional edges, dynamic Send API for parallel fan-out, human-in-the-loop via interrupt(), and node caching via CachePolicy. The functional API offers a lighter-weight alternative with @task and @entrypoint decorators.

title: Playwright MCP: Model Context Protocol Server for Browser Automation
description: Microsoft's official MCP server for Playwright enables LLMs to control browsers through structured accessibility snapshots rather than screenshots, making interactions deterministic and token-efficient. Supports 30+ tools including browser_navigate, browser_click, browser_type, browser_snapshot, and browser_fill_form, with Docker support and network mocking.

title: Orchestrator-Worker Pattern for Dynamic Task Decomposition
description: The orchestrator-worker pattern has a central LLM orchestrator break tasks into subtasks, delegate them to worker LLMs for parallel execution, then synthesize results. Workers can use different models or tools. LangGraph's Send API enables dynamic fan-out where the number of workers is determined at runtime based on the input.

title: MCP Code Mode: Progressive Tool Disclosure for Token-Efficient Agents
description: Instead of loading all tool definitions into context (expensive at scale), MCP Code Mode lets agents write code to call tools via generated API wrappers. This enables progressive disclosure (load only needed tools), context-efficient results (filter data before returning), and efficient control flow (loops/conditionals as code, not chained LLM calls). Reduces token consumption by up to 98.7%.

title: LangGraph Multi-Agent Systems: Supervisor, Swarm, and Pipeline Patterns
description: A deep dive into three canonical multi-agent architectures in LangGraph: Supervisor (centralized router with Send API for parallel fan-out), Swarm (decentralized peer-to-peer handoffs via Command(goto=)), and Pipeline (sequential fixed stages). Mathematical analysis shows 3 specialized agents each 20% better than a generalist yield 73% compound quality improvement.

title: Synapse: Spreading Activation for Episodic-Semantic Memory in Agents
description: Synapse implements a unified episodic-semantic memory graph with spreading activation, lateral inhibition, and temporal decay. It combines semantic similarity, activation-based traversal, and PageRank structural priors for retrieval. Achieves 40.5 average F1 on LoCoMo, with 96.6 on adversarial queries — dramatically outperforming A-Mem and Zep.

title: Kumiho: Graph-Native Cognitive Memory with AGM Belief Revision
description: Kumiho implements a property graph memory architecture with AGM belief revision semantics, supporting working, episodic, semantic, procedural, and associative memory types. Immutable version chains with Supersedes edges enable principled belief updates. Achieves 93.3% on LoCoMo-Plus (vs. 45.7% best baseline) and 98.5% recall accuracy.

title: CraniMem: Cranial-Inspired Gated and Bounded Memory for Agents
description: CraniMem implements a dual-store architecture (bounded episodic buffer + knowledge graph) with goal-conditioned gating and consolidation loops. Its noise-robustness is best-in-class: only 0.011 F1 drop under noise injection (vs. 0.027 for vanilla RAG). Goal-directed gating prevents distractors from entering memory.

title: BrainMem: Brain-Inspired Evolving Memory for Embodied Agent Task Planning
description: BrainMem implements a three-tier memory hierarchy (working, episodic, semantic) with guided-retry for failed tasks, utility scoring for semantic guideline pruning, and episode-level consolidation via LLM summarization. Achieves 75.0% success on EB-ALFRED (up from 56.3% without memory), with the largest gains on long-horizon and spatial tasks.

title: Hierarchical Memory Theory for Language Agents (CoALA Framework)
description: A unifying formal theory for hierarchical memory systems using three operators: Extraction (raw data to atomic units), Coarsening (partitioning with representatives), and Traversal (query-directed retrieval). Introduces the self-sufficiency spectrum and Coarsening-Traversal coupling principle, mapping 11 existing systems including RAPTOR, GraphRAG, xMemory, and H-MEM.

title: Pre-Act: Multi-Step Planning Before Action Outperforms ReAct
description: Pre-Act extends ReAct by generating a complete multi-step execution plan with detailed reasoning before acting, then incrementally refining the plan after each step. Outperforms ReAct by 70% in action recall on Almita. A fine-tuned Llama 3.1 70B model outperforms GPT-4, showing smaller models can match larger ones with proper training.

title: Planner-Centric DAG Framework: Decoupling Planning from Execution
description: Decouples planning from execution by having a dedicated Planner model generate a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) for the execution plan, enabling parallel tool execution. Uses hierarchical rewards (GRPO) that penalize structural errors and reward edge F1. A Qwen3-8B planner achieves 59.8% SoPR on StableToolBench, outperforming GPT-4 ReAct at 48.2%, with only 2.29 average inference steps.

title: THOUGHTSCULPT: Reasoning with Intermediate Revision via MCTS
description: THOUGHTSCULPT extends Tree of Thought by allowing nodes to be revised (not just extended) via Monte Carlo Tree Search with a self-revision mechanism. Uses three modules: Thought Evaluator (holistic + itemized), Thought Generator, and Decision Simulator. Achieves +30% interestingness on story outlines and +16% word success on mini-crosswords with only 20 steps vs. ToT's 100.

title: CrewAI Multi-Agent Debate: Proposer-Opposer-Judge Pattern
description: A CrewAI project implementing a three-agent debate system using different LLMs per agent role (Claude for Proposer, GPT-4 for Opposer, Gemini for Judge). YAML-configured with sequential processing, this pattern is natural for verification, adversarial testing, and improving output quality through structured disagreement.

title: Multi-Agent Debate Arena with Structured Argument Cards (Python/FastAPI)
description: A full-stack debate arena (FastAPI + Next.js + CrewAI) where agent responses are abstracted into argument cards with move types (Attack, Defense, Refute), power levels, and three-judge evaluation. Includes web-grounded reasoning via DuckDuckGo, real-time SSE streaming, and Dockerized deployment.
code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool

search = SerperDevTool()

proposer = Agent(
    role="Proposer",
    goal="Argue for the motion with evidence and logic",
    backstory="Expert debater who constructs compelling arguments.",
    tools=[search],
    llm="claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
)

opposer = Agent(
    role="Opposer",
    goal="Argue against the motion, exposing weaknesses",
    backstory="Critical thinker who finds flaws in arguments.",
    tools=[search],
    llm="gpt-4o",
)

judge = Agent(
    role="Judge",
    goal="Evaluate both sides and deliver a verdict",
    backstory="Impartial arbiter with deep knowledge.",
    llm="gemini/gemini-2.5-pro",
)

propose_task = Task(
    description="Argue for: {motion}",
    expected_output="Structured argument with claims, evidence, and citations.",
    agent=proposer,
)

oppose_task = Task(
    description="Argue against: {motion}",
    expected_output="Counter-argument rebutting the proposer's claims.",
    agent=opposer,
)

verdict_task = Task(
    description="Evaluate both arguments and deliver a verdict.",
    expected_output="Winner, reasoning, and scoring rubric.",
    agent=judge,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[proposer, opposer, judge],
    tasks=[propose_task, oppose_task, verdict_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"motion": "Autonomous AI agents should have self-correction loops"})

title: Claude Code Subagents: Delegated Workers with Custom Tools and Models
description: Claude Code subagents are configurable via Markdown files with YAML frontmatter, supporting custom tools, models, permissions, hooks, MCP servers, skills, and memory scopes. Built-in subagents include Explore (Haiku, read-only) and Plan (research). Forked subagents (experimental) inherit full conversation context, sharing the prompt cache with the parent session for efficiency.

title: Claude Code Agent Teams: Multi-Agent Orchestration with Shared Task Lists
description: Agent Teams enable one lead and multiple teammates, each with a full context window. They share a task list with dependency tracking and file locking for race conditions. Teammates communicate via mailboxes (not just through the lead). Token cost is significant since each teammate is a separate Claude instance.

title: Running Parallel Claude Code Agents with Worktrees
description: Practical patterns for running 2-4 parallel Claude Code sessions using git worktrees for filesystem isolation. Each worktree gets its own directory, branch, and zero file interference. Key patterns: alias cw='claude --worktree' for quick access, ci-$JOB_ID per job for CI/CD isolation, and one orchestrator dispatching one worktree per migration target.

title: AutoGPT Platform: Visual Agent Builder with Autonomous Mode
description: AutoGPT has evolved from a CLI tool into a full agent platform with visual workflow editor, marketplace, and cloud hosting. The SmartDecisionMakerBlock now supports agent_mode_max_iterations for autonomous tool execution loops (0=single-call, N=limited iterations, -1=infinite). Supports OpenAI, Claude, LLaMA, and other LLM providers.

title: AutoGPT Agent Mode: Autonomous Tool Execution Loops in the Platform
description: AutoGPT's SmartDecisionMakerBlock now supports agent_mode_max_iterations for autonomous multi-step tool execution. Setting it to 0 gives traditional single-call mode, 1+ enables agent mode with iteration limits, and -1 enables infinite autonomous mode. Includes proper database operations, Pydantic type safety, and comprehensive tests.

title: Demystifying LLM-Based Software Engineering Agents (FSE 2025)
description: A landmark paper arguing that simple non-agent approaches can match complex agents. Agentless uses a 3-phase pipeline (localization, repair, validation) without autonomous tool use, achieving 32% on SWE-bench Lite at $0.70 avg cost. Key insight: agent-based approaches suffer from complex tool usage errors and limited self-reflection. Adopted by OpenAI and DeepSeek for evaluations.

title: OpenHands SDK V1: Event-Sourced State Management for Self-Debugging Agents
description: OpenHands SDK V1 (MLSys 2026) introduces event-sourced state management with sub-millisecond persist latency, 61% fewer system-attributable failures, and MCP integration. Supports multi-LLM routing, context condensation (2x cost reduction), security analyzer for action risk assessment, and action confirmation policies. Achieves 76.6% on SWE-Bench Verified with Opus 4.5.

title: PAR2-RAG: Planned Active Retrieval and Reasoning for Multi-Hop QA
description: PAR2-RAG separates coverage (breadth-first anchoring) from commitment (depth-first iterative refinement with an Evidence Sufficiency Controller). Achieves 23.5% higher accuracy over IRCoT on MHQA benchmarks with 10.5% NDCG retrieval gains. Design principle: retrieve broadly before committing narrowly.

title: DualRAG: Dual-Process Reasoning and Retrieval Integration
description: DualRAG couples Reasoning-augmented Querying (RaQ) that dynamically identifies knowledge gaps and generates targeted queries with progressive Knowledge Aggregation (pKA) that organizes retrieved info into evolving outlines. Approaches oracle-knowledge performance on HotpotQA and 2wikiMultiHopQA while using only 1.6-2.2 retrieval iterations vs. IRCoT's 3.7-4.3.

title: ACE: Agentic Context Evolution — Metacognitive To Retrieve or To Think
description: ACE uses a multi-agent orchestrator with majority voting to dynamically decide between RETRIEVE (external search) and THINK (internal reasoning/sub-query generation) at each step. Achieves 62.8% on HotpotQA (vs. 38.9% for vanilla RAG) using 42% fewer tokens than brute-force IterDRAG, avoiding contextual saturation from over-retrieval.

title: SPD-RAG: Sub-Agent Per Document for Cross-Document Synthesis
description: SPD-RAG assigns a dedicated sub-agent per document for exhaustive extraction, then merges findings via similarity-ordered recursive synthesis. Achieves 58.1 average score on LOONG (vs. 33.0 for Normal RAG and 32.8 for Agentic RAG) at only 38% of the API cost of the full-context baseline, with +40.5 points on clustering tasks.

title: Osprey: Production-Ready Agentic AI for Safety-Critical Control Systems
description: Osprey deploys agentic AI at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Advanced Light Source, managing hundreds of thousands of control channels. Key patterns: plan-first orchestrator generates complete execution plans for human review before any action, capability classification layer prevents prompt inflation, and protocol-agnostic connectors enable graceful degradation across mock and production environments.

title: VIGIL: Reflective Runtime for Self-Healing LLM Agents
description: VIGIL is a meta-agent that watches task agents and performs autonomous maintenance through EmoBank (affective memory with exponential decay for detecting soft failures), stage-gated pipelines that enforce strict execution order, and fallback logic that demonstrates meta-procedural self-repair when its own diagnostic tools fail.

title: HASHIRU: Hierarchical Agent System for Hybrid Intelligent Resource Utilization
description: HASHIRU implements a CEO-Employee hierarchy with an economic model (hiring cost + invocation cost + expense cost) for explicit budget management. Prioritizes smaller local LLMs (3B-7B via Ollama) while flexibly using external APIs for complex tasks. Demonstrates autonomous budget refusal when allocation is exceeded.

title: Sovereign-OS: Constitution-First AI Orchestration with CFO-Enforced Budgets
description: A production-ready governance layer where one YAML charter defines mission, budget, KPIs, and capabilities. A CFO agent enforces max_task_cost_usd, daily_budget_usd, and min_job_margin_ratio before any task runs. TrustScore-gated permissions start agents sandboxed and earn capabilities through passing audits. Append-only ledger tracks every cent and token.

title: LangGraph ReAct Agent Tutorial: Build from Scratch with v1.0 API
description: A comprehensive tutorial on building a ReAct agent from scratch using LangGraph v1.0, covering the migration from deprecated v0.1 APIs (set_entry_point -> add_edge(START, node)), ToolNode for parallel tool execution, MessagesState, and conditional routing. Includes common pitfalls like forgetting the tools-to-agent edge and shipping MemorySaver to production.

title: LangGraph Tutorial by OpnCrafter: Cycles, Persistence, and Human-in-the-Loop
description: A practical tutorial emphasizing that linear chains are insufficient for production agents — cycles via LangGraph are essential. Covers human-in-the-loop with interrupt_before, parallel node execution, persistence and time-travel with checkpointers, and recursion_limit as an essential safety guard (set to N_expected_hops * 2).

title: LangGraph create_agent: High-Level Agent Factory with Middleware (Python)
description: LangChain 1.0's create_agent is a production-ready agent factory supporting static or dynamic model selection, middleware for wrapping model/tool calls, structured output via ToolStrategy or ProviderStrategy, and context schema for per-run data. Middleware hooks include @wrap_model_call, @wrap_tool_call, @before_model, @after_model, and @dynamic_prompt.

title: Deep Agent Pattern: TODO Lists, Context Offloading, and Sub-Agent Delegation
description: The Deep Agent pattern from LangChain Academy addresses the three main bottlenecks in autonomous agents: task drift (solved by TODO lists with status tracking and recitation), context overflow (solved by offloading to virtual file systems with ls/read/write operations in agent state), and context pollution (solved by spawning sub-agents with focused tool sets).
workflow: PLAN: Break the task into a TODO list with status fields (pending, in_progress, done); recite the list at the start of every LLM call to prevent task drift
OFFLOAD: When context fills up, write intermediate results to a virtual file system (ls/read_file/write_file/edit_file) stored in agent state rather than keeping everything in the conversation
DELEGATE: For subtasks that would pollute context (web search, code execution, file analysis), spawn a sub-agent with a focused tool set and isolated context; the parent receives only the summary
EXECUTE: The main agent loop reads the TODO list, picks the next pending item, executes it using tools or delegation, updates the status, and recites the updated list
VERIFY: After all TODO items are marked done, the agent reviews the full output for coherence and completeness before returning the final answer

title: Anthropic MCP: 10,000+ Community Servers and Registry API for Dynamic Tool Discovery
description: The Model Context Protocol has grown to 10,000+ community-built servers enabling LLMs to connect to external tools, data sources, and APIs. The upcoming Registry API will enable agents to dynamically search for and discover new MCP servers on demand — a key enabler for autonomous tool acquisition. New features include Elicitation (server-initiated user input) and Tasks (durable execution wrappers).

title: Agentic AI with LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen and BeeAI (IBM/Coursera)
description: Intermediate Coursera course by IBM covering multi-agent orchestration with LangGraph, CrewAI, BeeAI, and AG2/AutoGen. Includes agentic workflows, context management, and tool calling patterns.

title: Google Codelab: Scale Agents with CrewAI, LangGraph, A2A, and ADK
description: Hands-on codelab demonstrating cross-framework agent orchestration for a retail inventory restock system. Shows A2A protocol for inter-agent communication and Google ADK as orchestrator.

title: AI Agent Frameworks Compared: CrewAI vs LangGraph vs AutoGen vs Claude Code
description: 2026 comparison covering architecture, learning curve, multi-agent patterns, production readiness, and best use cases. Includes cross-framework orchestration strategies.

title: MIA: Memory Intelligence Agent with Manager-Planner-Executor Architecture
description: A Manager-Planner-Executor architecture integrating non-parametric memory (compressed trajectories) with parametric memory (search plans updated via test-time learning). Uses alternating RL training between Planner and Executor, plus reflection with unsupervised judgment for self-correction. Boosts GPT-5.4 by up to 9% on LiveVQA; a Qwen2.5-VL-7B executor with MIA outperforms the 32B model by 18%.
workflow: MANAGE: Memory Manager retrieves compressed historical trajectories relevant to the current task
PLAN: Planner generates a search plan using parametric memory, informed by retrieved trajectories
EXECUTE: Executor carries out the plan, producing intermediate results and observations
REFLECT: Unsupervised judgment module critiques the execution, identifying errors and inconsistencies
UPDATE: Bidirectional conversion loop updates both parametric (Planner weights) and non-parametric (Memory Manager) stores based on reflection results
ITERATE: Alternate RL training between Planner and Executor for synergistic co-evolution until convergence or max iterations

title: MemMA: Coordinating the Memory Cycle via Multi-Agent Reasoning and Self-Evolution
description: Addresses strategic blindness (forward path) and sparse feedback (backward path) in agent memory. A Meta-Thinker produces structured guidance steering a Memory Manager (construction) and Query Reasoner (iterative retrieval). Failed retrievals trigger in-situ self-evolving memory construction that synthesizes probe QA pairs and converts failures into repair actions before committing memory. Achieves 81.58% ACC on LoCoMo.
workflow: THINK: Meta-Thinker produces structured guidance based on the query context and task requirements
CONSTRUCT: Memory Manager builds new memories following the Meta-Thinker's guidance, forming structured representations
RETRIEVE: Query Reasoner performs iterative retrieval, refining queries based on intermediate results until sufficient evidence is gathered
VERIFY: If retrieval fails, synthesize probe QA pairs to test current memory coverage and identify gaps
EVOLVE: Convert verification failures into repair actions — update, merge, or create memories in-situ before committing
RESPOND: Generate the final answer using verified, evolved memories; store the interaction pattern for future meta-thinking cycles

title: MemRL: Self-Evolving Agents via Runtime Reinforcement Learning on Episodic Memory
description: Non-parametric RL approach enabling agent self-evolution without weight updates. Uses Intent-Experience-Utility triplet memory structure with two-phase retrieval (similarity-based recall then Q-value-based selection to filter semantic noise) and utility-driven updates via Monte Carlo-style Q-value updates from environmental feedback. Provides theoretical stability guarantees preventing catastrophic forgetting. Outperforms MemP by +3.8% across BigCodeBench, ALFWorld, Lifelong Agent Bench, and HLE.
workflow: ENCOD: Encode the current task as an intent vector capturing what the agent wants to achieve
RECALL: Retrieve candidate experiences via similarity-based matching against stored intent vectors
SELECT: Apply Q-value-based filtering on recalled candidates to prioritize high-utility experiences over merely similar ones
ACT: Execute the task using guidance from selected experiences, observing environmental feedback
UPDATE: Compute Monte Carlo-style Q-value updates from the feedback signal and update the experience triplet's utility score
STORE: Store the new Intent-Experience-Utility triplet; stability guarantees ensure no catastrophic forgetting of earlier high-utility memories

title: Polaris: Gödel Agent for Small Language Models via Experience-Abstracted Policy Repair
description: Recursive self-improvement for 7B-parameter models through experience-abstracted policy repair. Analyzes failures, synthesizes strategy patches as validated code-level policy modifications, and integrates them conservatively with bounded retries (up to 3 attempts). Controls context growth to make Gödel-style self-improvement feasible for SLMs. Achieves up to +9% improvement on MGSM, DROP, GPQA, and LitBench.
workflow: FAIL: Execute the current policy on a task and record the failure trace (inputs, outputs, error signals)
ANALYZE: Identify the root cause of failure from the trace — categorize as planning error, execution error, or reasoning gap
SYNTHESIZE: Generate a strategy-level patch that abstracts the failure pattern into a reusable policy modification
VALIDATE: Apply the patch to a held-out validation set with bounded retries (max 3 attempts); reject if it degrades performance
INTEGRATE: Merge validated patches into the base policy with conservative update rules that prevent context explosion
ITERATE: Re-execute the updated policy on new tasks, cycling through failure analysis and patch synthesis until convergence or iteration budget is exhausted

title: DeerFlow: Open-Source Long-Horizon SuperAgent Harness
description: ByteDance's open-source SuperAgent harness for long-horizon tasks that require research, coding, and creative generation. Orchestrates multiple specialized sub-agents (researcher, coder, writer) in a hierarchical pipeline with state persistence and progress tracking. Designed for complex multi-step workflows that exceed single-agent context windows.
workflow: DECOMPOSE: Break the long-horizon task into a sequence of sub-tasks (research, code, generate, review)
DISPATCH: Route each sub-task to the appropriate specialized sub-agent (researcher, coder, writer, reviewer)
EXECUTE: Each sub-agent processes its task using its own context and tools, producing intermediate artifacts
SYNTHESIZE: Aggregate sub-agent outputs into a coherent final result, resolving conflicts and redundancies
REVIEW: Evaluate the synthesized output against quality criteria; if insufficient, loop back to DISPATCH with refinement instructions
DELIVER: Present the final output to the user with full provenance tracing of each sub-agent's contribution

title: Agent Guide: Comprehensive AI Agent Development Guide with LangGraph and RAG
description: A structured 7-module guide covering AI agent development from fundamentals to production. Includes LangGraph hands-on, advanced RAG patterns, and transition guides from prototype to production deployment. Written in Chinese with code examples.
workflow: LEARN fundamentals: Agent patterns (ReAct, Plan-Execute), tool calling, and state management concepts
BUILD basic agent: Implement a ReAct agent with LangGraph StateGraph, nodes, and conditional edges
ADD tools: Integrate web search, database, and calculator tools with production safeguards (rate limiting, input validation)
IMPLEMENT memory: Add short-term (checkpointing) and long-term (vector store) memory for persistent conversations
APPLY advanced patterns: Human-in-the-loop interrupt/resume, multi-agent supervisor, and self-correction loops
HARDEN for production: Streaming responses, observability with LangSmith, cost tracking, and error recovery
DEPLOY: Containerize with FastAPI, set up Postgres checkpointing, configure Kubernetes HPA for horizontal scaling

title: A2A Protocol v1.0: Agent-to-Agent Communication and Task Lifecycle
description: The A2A Protocol v1.0 defining the open standard for agent-to-agent interoperability. Implements a 5-state task lifecycle (submitted, working, input-required, completed/failed/canceled/rejected) with Agent Cards for discovery, SSE streaming, and three protocol bindings (JSON-RPC, gRPC, REST). Governed by the Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation.
workflow: DISCOVER: Retrieve Agent Card from /.well-known/agent-card.json to identify capabilities, auth requirements, and supported task types
SUBMIT: Send a task to the remote agent with a unique task ID and optional Idempotency-Key for duplicate prevention
WORK: Remote agent processes the task, transitioning through working state with SSE streaming for progress updates
INPUT-REQUIRED: If the remote agent needs human input, pause and emit input-required state; orchestrator collects input and resumes
COMPLETE: Remote agent finishes and returns artifacts; task transitions to completed, failed, canceled, or rejected terminal state
RESOLVE: Orchestrator consumes the task result and artifacts, handling each terminal state appropriately (success, retry on failure, or escalate on rejection)

title: CrewAI Flows: Event-Driven Multi-Agent Orchestration with @start/@listen/@router
description: CrewAI Flows wrap Crews in an event-driven orchestration layer using @start (entry point), @listen (responds to events), and @router (conditional branching) decorators. Supports Pydantic state models, @persist for SQLite/PostgreSQL crash recovery, structured outputs, task guardrails, and the gradual autonomy pattern (start 100% human review, reduce threshold over time). Produces 14x less code than equivalent LangGraph implementations per DocuSign.
workflow: DEFINE state: Create a Pydantic model for structured, serializable flow state with type safety
START: Mark the entry method with @start() decorator — this is the first step triggered when the flow runs
LISTEN: Chain subsequent methods with @listen() decorators that react to outputs from previous steps
ROUTE: Use @router() for conditional branching — quality gates, approval workflows, or error handling paths
PERSIST: Add @persist decorator for SQLite (dev) or PostgreSQL (production) crash recovery with unique UUIDs per flow instance
GUARDRAIL: Apply task guardrails to validate outputs before passing to the next step; set max_iter on agents to prevent tool-call loops
DEPLOY: Use crewai deploy create after crewai login for managed infrastructure, or export to FastAPI with async kickoff

title: NirDiamant/agents-towards-production: End-to-End Production Agent Tutorials
description: A comprehensive collection of code-first tutorials for building production-grade GenAI agents. Covers the full journey from prototype to production including error handling, observability, cost management, evaluation, and deployment patterns. Over 20K stars on GitHub.
workflow: PROTOTYPE: Build a basic ReAct agent with tool calling and conversation memory
HARDEN: Add error handling, retry logic, input validation, and structured outputs with Pydantic
OBSERVE: Integrate LangSmith or OpenTelemetry for per-node tracing, cost tracking, and latency metrics
EVALUATE: Create evaluation datasets and run automated quality checks against expected outputs
PERSIST: Add database checkpointing (Postgres) for crash recovery and conversation continuity
SCALE: Containerize with Docker, set up horizontal scaling with shared state, and configure rate limiting
DEPLOY: Deploy to cloud with CI/CD, monitoring dashboards, and automated rollback procedures

title: AG2 Build: Sample Code and Application Showcases for AutoGen v0.13+
description: Official sample code repository for AG2 (formerly AutoGen) showcasing multi-agent patterns including group chat, swarm orchestration, tool integration, and human-in-the-loop workflows. Updated for the latest v0.13 API with LLMConfig and ConversableAgent.
workflow: INSTALL: Install AG2 with pip install ag2[openai] and configure LLMConfig with your API keys
CREATE: Define ConversableAgent instances with system messages, tool lists, and LLM configurations
ORCHESTRATE: Set up GroupChat or Swarm patterns for multi-agent collaboration with turn-based or speaker-selection rules
TOOL: Register @tool-decorated functions for web search, code execution, file I/O, and API access
HUMAN: Add human-in-the-loop with UserProxyAgent for approval gates and feedback collection
EXECUTE: Run the agent conversation with max_turns limits and timeout controls
INSPECT: Review conversation traces, token usage, and tool call logs for debugging and optimization

title: Agentok: Visual Multi-Agent Builder for AG2 (AutoGen)
description: A drag-and-drop visual tool for building multi-agent applications with AG2 (AutoGen). Provides a visual editor for defining agent roles, group chat configurations, and tool integrations without writing code, then generates executable AG2 configurations.
workflow: DESIGN: Drag and drop agent nodes onto the canvas, defining roles, system messages, and capabilities
CONNECT: Wire agents into group chat or swarm patterns using visual connectors
CONFIGURE: Set LLM configurations, tool integrations, and conversation parameters through property panels
TOOL: Assign @tool-decorated functions to agents from the built-in tool library or define custom tools
SIMULATE: Run test conversations in the visual debugger to verify agent interactions and tool usage
EXPORT: Generate executable AG2 Python configuration code from the visual design
DEPLOY: Use the exported configuration in production with AG2 runtime

title: Cordum: Open Agent Control Plane for Governing Autonomous AI Agents
description: An open agent control plane that governs autonomous AI agents with pre-execution policies. Provides policy-as-code guardrails, approval gates, audit logging, and runtime enforcement for agent actions before they execute. Designed for enterprise compliance and safety.
workflow: DEFINE: Write pre-execution policies as code specifying allowed actions, rate limits, and approval requirements
REGISTER: Register agents with the control plane, declaring their capabilities and policy scopes
INTERCEPT: Before each agent action, the control plane intercepts the request and evaluates it against applicable policies
APPROVE: If the action requires human approval, pause and route to the appropriate approver; otherwise auto-approve if within policy bounds
ENFORCE: Execute approved actions with runtime monitoring for policy violations or unexpected behavior
AUDIT: Log all decisions (approved, rejected, modified) with full context for compliance review and incident investigation

title: MCP Memory Service: Persistent Memory for AI Agent Pipelines
description: Open-source persistent memory service for AI agent pipelines (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen). Uses Knowledge Graph-based storage with semantic search, entity extraction, and relationship tracking. Provides MCP-compatible API for integration with any agent framework.
workflow: CONNECT: Agent pipeline connects to the MCP memory service via the Model Context Protocol interface
EXTRACT: Service extracts entities, relationships, and key facts from agent interactions and task results
STORE: Persist extracted knowledge in a Knowledge Graph with vector embeddings for semantic search
RECALL: When an agent needs context, query the memory service with natural language or structured filters
SEARCH: Perform semantic search across stored memories to find relevant past experiences and learned patterns
EVOLVE: Update and merge memories as new information arrives, maintaining consistency and resolving conflicts
INTEGRATE: Works with LangGraph checkpointing, CrewAI memory, and AutoGen group chat as a unified memory layer

title: SurfSense: Open-Source Privacy-Focused Alternative to NotebookLM for Teams
description: An open-source, privacy-focused research agent that combines web browsing, document analysis, and knowledge synthesis. Built with LangGraph for agentic workflows, it can research topics, synthesize information from multiple sources, and generate structured outputs while keeping data private.
workflow: INGEST: Upload documents, connect web sources, or let the agent browse for relevant information
RESEARCH: Agent autonomously searches, browses, and extracts information from multiple web and local sources
SYNTHESIZE: Combine findings across sources using LangGraph-orchestrated reasoning and fact-checking sub-agents
ANALYZE: Apply structured analysis (summary, comparison, timeline, entity extraction) to synthesized information
RESPOND: Generate privacy-preserving outputs (reports, answers, visualizations) with full source attribution
ITERATE: User can ask follow-up questions; agent uses conversation memory and prior research to refine answers
